titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sir Terence Clark photograph collection AD.MC.148
titlestmt.author: Brad Bauer

publicationstmt.publisher: NYU Abu Dhabi, Archives and Special Collections

address.addressline: New York University Abu Dhabi, C-2

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-02-27 15:50:56 +0400.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: NYU Abu Dhabi, Archives and Special Collections

did.unittitle: Sir Terence Clark photograph collection

physdesc.extent: in 1.5 file boxes and 9 photograph albums, comprising 1,064 photographic prints and 632 photographic slides (35 mm), as well as several folders of related ephemera.

did.abstract: Photographic prints, slides, and ephemera, related to the career of British diplomat Sir Terence Clark, and documenting people, places, and activities during his tenures as Assistant Political Agent at the Political Agency of Great Britain in Dubai (1965-1968), as Head of the Chancery at the British Embassy in Muscat (1972-1973), and as Ambassador to the Sultanate of Oman (1990-1994).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions governing access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for use in the Reading Room of the Archives and Special Collections department of the NYU Abu Dhabi Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate source of acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by purchase, in two increments, March and November 2025.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged largely by format. Prints that are housed in the albums have remained in their original order, and are described in the finding aid at the page level. The loose photographic prints and slides in the first series ("Dubai and the Trucial States") contain the same images, simply in different formats, with the slides arranged in roughly chronological order and the prints arranged by location and topic. The slides in the second series ("Oman") duplicate some of the content of the photo albums, and are arranged in roughly chronological order, divided into sub-series ("Places," "People," "Thesiger") that were arranged by the creator of the collection. The albums in this series are also arranged in chronological order, and descriptions of the contents that are in quotation marks represent captions written on the pages of the albums by the creator of the collection.

bioghist.head: Biographical note
bioghist.p: Sir Terence J. Clark, KBE, CMG, CVO, served as a British diplomat in multiple postings between 1955 and his retirement in 1994. He was born on 12 June 1934, and after attending Thomas Parmiter's School in London, he joined the Royal Air Force, serving from 1953-1955. During that time, he studied Russian at the School of Slavonic Studies at Cambridge, and after he joined the Foreign Service, he studied Arabic at the School of Oriental and African Studies (SOAS)in London, and then the Middle East Center for Arabic Studies (MECAS) in Lebanon from 1956-1957.

While he served in a variety of postings throughout his diplomatic career, most of the emphasis was on the Middle East and North Africa, which led him to postings in Bahrain (1957-58); Amman (1958-60); Casablanca (1961-62); Dubai (1965-1968), where he served as Assistant Political Agent; Tripoli [Libya] (February-March 1981), where he served as Chargé d'Affaires; and Muscat [Oman], where he was Head of Chancery (1972-1973). His career culminated in appointments as Ambassador to Iraq (1985-1989) and Ambassador to Oman (1990-1994). Following his retirement from the Foreign Service, he remained active as the Chair of the British-Omani Society from 1995-2004, as director of the Middle East Association, and as a senior consultant at Middle East Consultants. He also served as director of the International Crisis Group's Sarajevo project in 1996.
Sources: webpage for the British Omani Society, accessed in March 2025: https://www.britishomani.org/team/sir-terence-clark

Description of transcript of interview with Sir Terence Clark, 2002, British Diplomatic Oral History Programme, Churchill Archives Centre, University of Cambridge, accessed March 2025: https://archivesearch.lib.cam.ac.uk/repositories/9/archival_objects/431490

prefercite.head: Preferred citation
prefercite.p: Sir Terence Clark photograph collection; MC.148; box number; folder number or item identifier; Archives and Special Collections, New York University Abu Dhabi Library.

processinfo.head: Processing information
processinfo.p: Processed by Brad Bauer, July 2025 and January-February 2026.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Sir Terence Clark photograph collection is divided thematically and chronologically between two distinct periods of his diplomatic career, with most of the photographs originating from his postings in Dubai (1965-1968) and Muscat (1972-1973, 1990-1994).

The images from his time based in Dubai consist of photographic prints (61) mounted in one album, as well as color photographic slides (98), and copy prints, depicting scenes and events in the emirates of Dubai, Sharjah, Fujairah, and Ras al Khaimah, dating from Clark's tenure as an Assistant Political Agent assigned to the Political Agency of Great Britain in Dubai, from 1965 to 1968.

The album contains many images of meetings with various political leaders and dignitaries, including the ruler of Dubai, Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum, rulers of neighboring emirates, such as Abu Dhabi, Ajman, Umm al-Qawain, Ras al-Khaimah, Sharjah, and Fujairah, as well as King Hussein of Jordan, and various British diplomats stationed in the Gulf region. A number of the images were taken during the visit of King Hussein of Jordan to Dubai in 1967, and several of the images of leaders of the various Emirates of the Trucial States may have also originated from this event. Other images depict meetings between the staff of the British Political Agency and the leader of Dubai, Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum, as well as the opening of the Dubai Zoo in 1967. Many of the photographs were taken by official photographers, with some containing the attribution on the verso of the images of the agency Photo Press International, and the photographer Noor Ali Rashid. Others are unattributed and may have been taken by Clark, such as the images on Dubai Creek, or by staff of the Political Agency.

The color slides, while including a few images of Dubai, chiefly document visits to the east coast of the Trucial States, in particular the towns of Khor Fakkan and Dibba, and other locations in the emirate of Fujairah. Many images depict fishermen bringing in their catch, as well as various types of boats used in and around the town of Khor Fakkan. Other images focus on villagers and farmers, either on the east coast, or the inland areas of Sharjah emirate. A couple of images also depict Al Jahili fort in Al Ain (Buraimi), at that time used as a base for the Trucial Oman Scouts.

The second, and larger, group of photographs date chiefly from his last diplomatic posting, as Ambassador to the Sultanate of Oman, from 1990-1994, although this series also includes some photographs from his earlier posting to Muscat, as head of the Chancery from 1972-1973. These are housed in 8 photograph albums, plus one box of 534 photographic slides (mostly color), which have been rehoused into sleeves and folders. The first two albums date from Clark's assignment in Muscat in 1972-1973, while the remaining six albums document activities during his tenure as Ambassador twenty years later. During both postings, Ambassador and Mrs. Clark and various friends and colleagues enjoyed traveling around the country, visiting locales as varied as Sur and Ras al-Hadd, Jebel Akhdar, Musandam and Dhofar.

During his latter assignment in Muscat, Ambassador Clark hosted a number of visiting dignitaries, and some of these visits are documented in these albums, such as the visit of writer and explorer Wilfred Thesiger, on the occasion of the opening of an exhibition of his photographs in Muscat in October 1991; as well as visits of Prince Philip, the Duke of Edinburgh, in November 1991; and former Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher in April 1992. One noteworthy part of the Thesiger visit was a reunion that Ambassador Clark had arranged between Thesiger and the three men who had accompanied him on the voyages through the Empty Quarter that he undertook between 1946-1948, which he later described in his book, "Arabian Sands": Salim bin Ghubaisha, Salim Bin Kabina, and Musallim bin Tafl. A month after Thesiger's visit, Ambassador Clark traveled to Dhofar to visit Salim bin Kabina and his family at their home in al-Qafah, and Musallim bin Tafl in Mughshin.

Delegations of military leaders were frequent visitors as well, including General Sir Peter de la Billière, Sir Peter Harding (Marshal of the Royal Air Force), Gen. Michael Wilkes, Defence Secretary Malcolm Rifkind, and former Defence Secretary Tom King, and his wife Jane, among others. Many of these military delegations traveled to Dhofar, underscoring the continued strategic relevance of this region to British forces in the decades following the end of the Dhofar Rebellion.

One striking feature of the albums are its depictions of ordinary Omani citizens who the Clarks visited on their multiple trips throughout the Sultanate, from fishermen in Sur and Jebali tribesmen in the mountains above Salalah to traditional weavers in Sur, as well as various festivals, camel races, and Eid celebrations to which they were invited. Also depicted are camping and hiking expeditions in the Wahibah Sands, Ras al-Hadd, Jebel Akhdar and Jebel Shams, and a number of explorations of archaeologial sites throughout the country.

In the last album, Ambassador Clark focused on documenting the British Embassy, Chancery and official residence in old Muscat, all situated in a structure adjacent to al-Jalali fort and Al Alam, the Sultan's ceremonial palace. This structure was built in 1890, as the headquarters of the British Resident in Muscat, and served as the British Embassy for the last twenty years of its existence, until it was demolished in the mid-1990s, to make way for the expansion of Al Alam Palace. Just prior to that, the British Embassy moved to a newly-built structure in the diplomatic quarter of al-Khuwair, in the newer part of the city, in 1994, and this album includes images of that structure as well.

userestrict.head: Conditions governing use
userestrict.p: Rights to the photographs in this collection that were created by Sir Terence Clark have been transferred to NYU Abu Dhabi. Other images produced by third parties in this collection may be protected by copyright and/or related rights. You are free to use materials in the collection in any way that is permitted by the copyright and related rights legislation that applies to your use. For other uses you need to obtain permission from the rights-holder(s).

relatedmaterial.head: Related materials
relatedmaterial.p: The Archives and Special Collections department at the NYU Abu Dhabi Library also contains three separate items related to Wilfred Thesiger's visit to Muscat in October 1991. These include presentation copies of Thesiger's books "Arabian Sands" (DS208 .T48 1959) and "The Life of My Choice" (G525 .T415 1987), both inscribed by Thesiger to Ambassador Clark, as well as the exhibition catalog "Arabian Sands : exhibition of photographs" (TR647 .T442 A4 1991 Box), containing a foreword written by Clark, and inscribed by Thesiger. Enclosed with the copy of "The Life of My Choice" are a photograph of Thesiger, with Ambassador Clark and his wife, Liese, at lunch in the embassy, Muscat, and a letter from Thesiger to Lady Clark, dated 24 October 1991, thanking her for her hospitality during his visit.

controlaccess.geogname: United Arab Emirates -- Kings and rulers.

did.unittitle: Dubai and Trucial States
Photo album
Terence Clark, holding monkey, walking with Sheikh Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani, ruler of Qatar, at opening of Dubai Zoo
Visit of King Hussein of Jordan (3 images)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Images: 1. King Hussein posing with rulers of the Emirates of Ras al Khaimah, Umm al Qawain, Fujairah, Ajman, and Sharjah. 2. Sheikh Ahmad bin Rashid al Mualla (ruler of Umm al Qawain) and Sheikh Khalid bin Muhammad al Qasimi (ruler of Sharjah). 3. Sheikh Saqr bin Muhammad al Qasimi (ruler of Ras al Khaimah), Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan al Nahyan (ruler of Abu Dhabi) and Terence Clark.

did.unittitle: Various (3 images)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Images (top to bottom of page): 1. Seated, left to right, Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum (ruler of Dubai), David Roberts (Political Agent, Dubai), Sheikh Muhammad bin Hamad al Sharqi (ruler of Fujairah), Terence Clark (Asst. Political Agent). 2. Walking down stairs, left to right, Terence Clark, David Roberts, Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum. 3. Seated around table, left to right, Major Tim Budd (Trucial Oman Scouts), Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum, Terence Clark, Colonel Pat Ive (COMTOS), and standing, on right, is Aminallah Farooghi, Headmaster, Iranian School, Dubai.

did.unittitle: Arrival for the opening of the Dubai Zoo

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images, top to bottom of page: 1. left to right, Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum (ruler of Dubai), entering zoo with Sheikh Ahmad bin 'Ali al Thani (ruler of Qatar) and Otto Bulart (director of zoo). 2. Group at entrance of zoo. 3. left to right, Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum (partially obscured), Terence Clark, Sheikh Ahmad bin 'Ali al Thani.

did.unittitle: Unidentified event [possibly during visit of King Hussein], ruler of Dubai and others (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page: 1. left to right, Sheikh Saqr bin Muhammad al Qasimi (ruler of Ras al Khaimah), Sheikh Khalid bin Saqr al Qasimi, Trevor Bevan (commandant, Ras al Khaimah Police). 2. Sheikh Khalid bin Saqr al Qasimi, Major David Neild (Trucial Oman Scouts), Yvonne Keirl (British Bank of the Middle East), Trevor Bevan, Tim Ashe (D.I.O., Ras al Khaimah). 3. in foreground, Sheikh Saqr bin Muhammad al Qasimi, in background, Robert Mackay (Digdaga Research Farm), Trevor Bevan, Tim Ashe, Chris Keirl (British Bank of the Middle East), Margaret Mackay.

did.unittitle: Unidentified event [possibly visit of King Hussein], ruler of Dubai and others (2 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom: 1. Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum (Dubai) with Sheikh Khalid bin Muhammad al Qasimi (Sharjah). 2. Terence Clark seated next to Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum, with Amin Farooghi (Iranian School, Dubai) standing behind them.

did.unittitle: Unidentified event [possibly visit of King Hussein], rulers of various Emirates and British diplomats (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom: 1. Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum (Dubai) speaking with David Roberts (Political Agent) and Terence Clark (Assistant Political Agent). 2. Rulers of Dubai, Ras al Khaimah, Sharjah and Abu Dhabi, conferring together. 3. Standing at table, left to right: Terence Clark, Sheikh Humaid bin Rashid al Nu'aimi (Ajman), Sheikh Khalid bin Muhammad al Qasimi (Sharjah), Sheikh Hamad bin Muhammad al Sharqi (Fujairah), Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan al Nahyan (Abu Dhabi), Sheikh Saqr bin Muhammad al Qasimi (Ras al Khaimah).

did.unittitle: Unidentified events (2 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : 1. Terence Clark, seated at table with Rashid Mabrooshi (Dubai Airport Immigration) and Sheikh Humaid bin Rashid al Nu'aimi (Ajman). 2. Terence Clark, seated with Muhammad Said, director of Kuwait State Office, Squadron Leader John Parker (commander of Royal Air Force Base in Sharjah), and others.

did.unittitle: Visit of King Hussein of Jordan to Dubai (2 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : Sheikh Rashid bin Said al Maktoum (ruler of Dubai) seated and speaking with King Hussein. 2. Standing, left to right, Terence Clark, David Roberts, Bill Duff (financial advisor to Sheikh Rashid bin Said), King Hussein.

did.unittitle: Visit of King Hussein of Jordan to Dubai (2 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : 1. Standing, left to right, Terence Clark, David Roberts, Bill Duff, King Hussein. 2. Terence Clark (Assistant Political Agent, Dubai) speaking with King Hussein of Jordan.

did.unittitle: Scenes from Dubai Creek (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : 1. Friday Mosque, behind wind towers. 2. Captain's stores. 3. Dubai Customs, Sheikh Rashid's counting house.

did.unittitle: Scenes from Dubai Creek (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : Ruler of Dubai's father's former house [Shindagha district]. 2. Bridge across Creek. 3. Souq wharf.

did.unittitle: Scenes from Dubai Creek (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top of page to bottom : 1. British Political Agency, Dubai. 2. Assistant Political Agent's house. 3. Abra (ferry) across Dubai Creek.

did.unittitle: Scenes from Dubai Creek (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page : 1. Boom [type of boat] at Darwish Steps. 2. Looking towards Carlton Hotel. 3. Dubai Customs.

did.unittitle: Ruler of Dubai training falcons at Za'abil Palace (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : unlabeled in album, but depict 1. Sheikh Rashid bin Said seated on bench, holding falcon. 2. Falcon handler standing, holding falcon, next to another man, with Land Rover in background. 3. Falcon handler holding falcon, while crouching on ground.

did.unittitle: Various images, Dubai (3)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page : 1. Unlabeled, depicting falcon handler with falcon on the ground. 2. Ruler of Dubai's yacht, "Za'abil." 3. Smugglers cook lunch on Zarawani's launch.

did.unittitle: East coast (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page : 1. Hamad bin Hamid, Wali of Husn Dibba, Sharjah. 2. Sharqi tribesman, Dibba Bay. 3. Abdullah bin Alai, Wali of Dibba, Fujairah, and son Muhammad.

did.unittitle: East coast (4 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom : 1. Dibba tribesman, Muhammad bin Abdallah, wali's son. 2. Muhammad bin Said of Muraba. 3. Sheikh Saif bin Muhammad, Wali of Fujairah. 4. Women drawing water, Fujairah.

did.unittitle: East coast (3 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page : 1. and 2. 'Ali, o/c Police, Khor Fakkan. 3. Wadi, near Masafi.

did.unittitle: Buraimi (4 images)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page : 1. and 2. Trucial Oman Scouts headquarters, Buraimi Fort [Al Jahili Fort, Al 'Ain]. 3. and 4. Camel souq, Buraimi [Al 'Ain].

did.unittitle: Terence Clark with various local personalities (3 images), including Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid al Nu'aimi (ruler of Ajman)

odd.head: Contents of page
odd.p: Images from top to bottom of page : 1. Standing, left to right, Head of Iranian Red Lion and Sun (unnamed), Amin Farooghi, Terence Clark, Sheikh Rashid bin Humain al Nu'aimi, unidentified, Sheikh Humaid bin Rashin al Nu'aimi. 2. Terence Clark with Kemal Hamza, Director, Dubai Municipality. 3. Terence Clark with 'Ali, the head of the Saudi Office, Dubai, and Ibrahim, from the Kuwait State Office, Dubai, and one unidentified person.

did.unittitle: Terence Clark greeting Sir William (Bill) Luce, Political Resident, Persian Gulf, at airfield (2 images)
Photographic slides
November - December 1965 (20 slides)

March 1966 (20 slides)
March 1966 - February 1967 (20 slides)
February 1967 - December 1968 (18 slides)
Prints (copies of slides)
Bedouin villagers (13 prints)
Dubai Creek (11 prints)
East coast : Dibba, Fujairah, and unidentified locales (15 prints)

Falconers and falcons (9 images)
Farmers and villagers, unidentified locales (4 images)

Various, including Sheikh Rashid bin Humaid al-Nu'aimi (ruler of Ajman), and seacraft (5)
Village scenes : Sharjah and unidentified locales
Oman
Photo albums
Album 1 : "Muscat, 1972"
Sultan Qaboos meeting with British Prime Minister Edward Heath and Foreign Secretary Alec Douglas-Home (2 images)
"Oman 1972-73" [scenes of Muscat](5 images)

[Diplomatic reception, various persons, Muscat?] (2 images)
Album 2 : "Muscat, 1972"
"Bandar Jissah, Chris Best" (6 images)
"Visit of Lord Balniel, Minister of State for Defence" [includes Major Richard Anderson, Tony Reeves, Ranulph Fiennes] (4 images)
"Visit to Sur" [includes Wali of Ibra] (5 images)
"Bilad bani bu Ali" (5 images)
"Bani bu Ali, Sur Creek, Wali at Sur" (6 images)
"Fishermen off Tiwi, sunrise near Tiwi" (5 images)
"Ghayl al Shab near Tiwi" (5 images)
"Ghayl al Shab" (5 images)
"Nizwa, Wali of Nizwa" (5 images)
"Rustaq suq, Nizwa suq, Tower of Nakhl Fort" (5 images)
"Askars at Sumail, Wali of Sumail" (5 images)
[unlabeled] (4 images)
"Near Quriyat, Rustaq, Near Ibri, Near Sohar" (6 images)
"Rustaq, Askars at Bahla, Bahla, Wadi Nakhl" (6 images)
"Oman 1973, Al Hazm" [loose page at end of album] (3 images)
[al Hazm] "Portuguese cannon" [loose page at end of album] (4 images)
Album 3 : "Oman, 1991"
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," includes Sultan's helicopter (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," at the Salalah Bird Sanctuary (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," with Muhammad bin Faraj al Ghassani, Dr. Robert Whitcombe (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," reception at Teetam, Jebel Dhofar, including images of three Jebalis, and Brian McGrath, personal secretary to Prince Philip (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," includes Jebalis at Teetam, headland with myrrh bushes (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," headland with myrrh bushes, Wadi Karshot (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," with Sheikh Ahmed Suhail, at Wadi Karshot (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," picnic al fresco at Aqabat al-Job (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," picnic al fresco, with Prince Philip and Ambassador Clark (2 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," picnic al fresco, with Prince Philip, Ambassador Clark, al-Ghassani, seated together on carpet (1 image)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," the party, including Prince Philip, drinking camel's milk (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," guests seated at al fresco picnic (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," group portrait (1 image)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," guests at al fresco picnic (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," guests at al fresco picnic (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," Wadi Darbat (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip, to Dhofar," includes images of fig tree, al-Ayoun pools, Khor Rori (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," Wadi Darbat, with Prince Philip, Ambassador Clark, al-Ghassani (2 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," departure, includes Prince Philip taking leave of Governor of Dhofar and other Omani officials (3 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," departure (2 images)
"Visit of Prince Philip to Dhofar," Prince Philip posing with flight crew and boarding royal jet (3 images)
"Picnic in Wadi Dhaiqa," with Donald Hawley, Joanna Owens, and others (3 images)

"Visit to the Wahaiba Sands," with image of group that included German and Swedish diplomats and their families, Wing Commander Ian Kendrick (R.A.F. Director of Music) and wife, and Omani guide, Salim (3 images)
[two Omani children, unidentified location, dress in local costume] (1 image)
[Omani man, loading a camel with goods, unidentified location, perhaps Wahibah Sands] (two images)
[Omani man, riding a camel loaded with goods, unidentified location, perhaps Wahibah Sands, plus one image of an Omani adolescent] (three images)
"Palace tea party on National Day," plus images of Omani adolescent (three images)
"Visit to Dhofar," including travel by helicopter to Habrut and Makinat Shahn (3 images)
"Visit to Dhofar: Makinat Shahn, Sarfait," includes image of Omani military personnel and vehicle (3 images)
"Visit to Dhofar : Shisur, Obar Expedition at Obar, possible caravanserai at Shisur," (3 images)
"Visit to Dhofar : trilith off the road, Thumrait to Mudai" (2 images)
[unidentified location, two images of local Omanis carrying goods on their heads, one of coastal cliffs shrouded in fog] (3 images)

Amb. Clark with delegation, including Ahmad Said al-Shanfari (Minister of Petroleum and Minerals) and Maqboot Ali Sultan (Minister of Commerce and Industry), plus image of Dr. Asim al-Jamali (former Minister of Health and Prime Minister), with Maimouna and Nabil, unidentified locations (2 images)
"At Makinat Shahn, Dhofar," Ambassador Clark pictured with Omani military personnel (3 images)
Album 4 : "Oman, 1991"

[Three events]: 1. Promotion for British Airways, with Amb. Clark, various staff, with Fatik bin Fahr al-Said, of the Omani ruling family, 2. "Farewell for the Dutch, Thai and Malaysian [diplomats?]," 3. Amb. Clark with Ranjit Gupta (Indian Ambassador to Oman), at luncheon (3 images)
"Wadi Bani Kharus," images of seated man, with spool of cloth (3 images)
"Awabi, Wadi Bani Kharus" (seated group of people, with Amb. Clark) and "Wadi al-Hijir" (3 images)
[Wadi al-Hijir], group with Mark Shieland (U.S. Embassy) and Mrs. Shieland (4 images)
"Wadi al Hijir," same group as in previous photos, with images of Saluki dog on leash, standing in water (3 images)
[Unlabeled] Older local man with children, and Amb. Clark, either Wadi al-Hijir or Wadi Bani Kharus (3 images)
"Wadi Bani Kharus," with image of stone dwelling at base of mountain, petroglyphs, and lizard (3 images)

Three different topics: 1. portrait of Amb. Clark with saluki dog, taken by Keith Machlachlan, 25 April 1991, 2. two Omani children at Wadi Hijir, undated; 3. and 4.: visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière, at Mirbat, Dhofar, 30 September 1991.

Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière, at Mirbat, Dhofar, various images, with Omanis, Amb. Clark, and Lady de la Billière, as well as site of bunker where two British SAS soldiers were killed during Battle of Mirbat in 1973 (6 images)
Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière, at Mirbat, Dhofar, various (6 images)
Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière, at Mirbat, Dhofar, various group images, including Maj. Gen. John Baskervyle-Glegg and his wife, Jane (5 images)
Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière, at Mirbat, Dhofar, various images, of group taking coffee break on beach, next to helicopter (6 images)

"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the "Arabian Sands" exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" image of Thesiger from 1945, plus three of Thesiger's traveling companions during his journey across the Empty Quarter, Salim bin Ghubaisha, Bin Kabina, and Musallim bin Tafl, as photographed on the terrace of the British Embassy in Muscat
"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" Thesiger with Bin Kabina, and Said, son of Bin al-Maman, at Wadi al-Muaydin, near Birkat al Mouz

"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" one image of Bin Ghubaisha at Wadi al-Muaydin, and three images of meeting with Thesiger, Amb. Clark, and Sayyid Fahr bin Taimur, Deputy Prime Minister for Security and Defence, at the latter's home (4 images)
"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" copies of Thesiger's photographs from exhibition (3 images)

"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" lunch at the British Ambassador's residence, Muscat, with Thesiger, Bin Kabina, Bin Ghubaisha, and Musallim bin Tafl (3 images)

"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" lunch at the British Ambassador's residence, Muscat, with Thesiger, Bin Kabina, Bin Ghubaisha, and Musallim bin Tafl (3 images)

"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" lunch at the British Ambassador's residence, Muscat, with Thesiger, Bin Kabina, Bin Ghubaisha, and Musallim bin Tafl (5 images)

"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" lunch at the British Ambassador's residence, Muscat, with Thesiger, Bin Kabina, Bin Ghubaisha, and Musallim bin Tafl (3 images)
"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" opening of exhibition at the Qurm Cultural Centre, Muscat, and lunch at Inshirah Restaurant
"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" visit to Sulaiman bin Himyar's fort at Birkat al Mouz (4 images)
"Visit of Wilfred Thesiger for the 'Arabian Sands' exhibition of his photographs of 1945-1950:" at Nizwa Fort and house of Sheikh Yasir's son as al-'Izz, and departure (4 images)
Visits of Amb. Clark to Sheikh Ahmad al-Easai at Sohar and Sheikh Sultan bin Muhammad bin Rahama al-Shamsi, with son Khalid, at Sunaina (3 images)
Visit of Amb. Clark (and Andrew Turner) to Sheikh Abdallah al-Qitabi, president of the Consultative Council at Mamour, and Sheikh Muhammad al-Qitabi, Minister of Electricity and Water (3 images)
"Visitors to the feast at al-Mamour" [Sheikh Abdallah al-Qitabi's house] (4 images)
Three images: 1. "Muscat bay on a misty morning in September," 2. "Sulaiman bin Himyar's fort (restored at Birkat al Mouz," 3. "Visit to the Jebel Akhdar, Shuraija" (3 images)
"Visit to the Jebel Akhdar": "Wadi Bani Habib, deserted village in Jebel Akhdar" (3 images)
"Visit to the Jebel Akhdar": Wadi Bani Habib resident (woman with 7 children) and unnamed Under-Secretary at Ministry of Agriculture and Fisheries (3 images)
Visit to al-Hazm fort, with Omani escort and guards pictured (3 images)
Visit to al-Hazm fort, with Director of Rumais Agricultural Center and Undersecretary of Ministry of Agriculture and Fisheries, guard at fort, and man with herd of racing camels (3 images)
"Visit to German archaeological dig at Samad al-Shan--Chalcolithic graves" (5 images)
"Salim bin Kabina at al-Qafah, Dhofar" (4 images)
"Salim bin Kabina at al-Qafah, Dhofar," including images of Bin Kabina with incense burner (4 images)
"Salim bin Kabina at al-Qafah, Dhofar," including Bin Kabina with women and children, and group of men including Muhammed bin Salim al-Masha'li (3 images)
"Salim bin Kabina at al-Qafah, Dhofar," with women and children (3 images)
"Musallim bin Tafl at Muqshin," two images of Musallim plus one of a woman from the previous group of photographs (3 images)
"Jebel Dhofar in the Monsoon (pictures by Muhammad Said al-Mashali):" landscapes and grazing camels (3 images)
"Jebel Dhofar in the Monsoon (pictures by Muhammad Said al-Mashali):" includes image of a Jebali house and a man drying sardines (3 images)
"Reception for England Under-16s [football], vs. Omani youth team": Amb. Clark greeting Omani players (3 images)

"The match (Oman 2-1)": includes Amb. Clark seated with Sir Bert Millichip (President, Football Association) and Ibrahim Subhi (acting President, Youth Sport Organisation) at the match, and reception for English team, with Sheikh Saud al-Rawahy, Air Commander Malallah, and Mrs. Clark, at Sayyid Fahr's (3 images)
"Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière", including Amb. Clark standing with General de la Billière outside of the tent of Sultan Qabus, at Jiddat al Harasis (3 images)
"Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière", including meeting with Sultan Qabus, along with Amb. Clark and General Sir Michael Wilkes (2 images)
"Visit of General Sir Peter de la Billière", one image depicting meeting with the general, Amb. Clark, and Sultan Qabus, in the latter's tent camp at Jiddat al-Harasis
"Visit to 'Obar' (Shisur), and to Adam": images with Margarethe Bertilsson, Rachel Curtis, Kemal Sultan, Juris Zarins, and Amb. Clark (5 images)
"Visit to the tombs east of Ibra" : with Marion Kendrick, Erwin Schurtenberger (Swiss diplomat) and others (3 images)
"Visit to the tombs east of Ibra" (3 images)
Bilad Bani Bu Ali: the fort and mosque (3 images)
Bilad Bani Bu Ali : mosque, group of visitors, including Jimmy Spiers, Pat and Trevan Hingston, Omani hosts, and saluki dog (3 images)
Visit to Sur : includes Arabian horses distributed by the Sultan, a wayfarer and his camel, excavations at Ras al-Hadd, and men careening a dhow (5 images)
Visit to Sur : traditional weaver at Sur and Arabian horses (3 images)
Album 5 : "Oman, 1992"
"Visit of Mrs. Thatcher" [former Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher, with Denis Thatcher and Dr. Omar Zawawi] (3 images)
"Visit of Mrs. Thatcher" (3 images)

"Visit of Mrs. Thatcher:" with Khalfan Nasr al-Wahaibi, Georgina Armour, Yusuf Alawi (Foreign Minister), Muhammad Musa (Secretary-General, Development Council), Yahya bin Mahfoodh al-Maudhari (Minister of Education)(3 images)
"Visit of Mrs. Thatcher:" former Prime Minister Thatcher, with Katya Landon, and one other (3 images)
Departure of Margaret Thatcher (1 image) and Faqeen fort near Manah (2 images)
"Lunch at Chequers:" Prime Minister John Major greeting Sultan Qabus, outside front entrance (1 image)
Flowers and shrubs, unidentified location [Jebel Shams?] (3 images)
Local vendors of textiles [en route to Jebel Shams?](3 images)
Group on excursion [en route to Jebel Shams?], including Deputy Chief of Mission of U.S. Embassy, Haywood Rankin, and others, with salukis, at Coleman's Rock (3 images)
"Village on Jebel Shams" and "Kuwait National Day," latter including diplomats at reception (3 images)
"Thai National Day" and "Kuwaiti National Day:" diplomats at reception, including Amb. Clark (3 images)
"The MENAS ship 'Relume'" and "Racing camels at Mintirib, Wahaiba Sands" (3 images)
"Wadi Aswad: Salima, wife of Sh. Saif b. Salim al-Dari and her children" (3 images)
Unlabeled [Wadi Aswad; Sh. Saif bin Salim al-Dari and two of his children] (3 images)
"Saluqi hunters at Bilad Bani Bu Ali" [men and dogs] (3 images)
Camping group, among prosopis trees at Wahaiba Sands, includes: Michael Stedman, Fanny Stedman, Denise Troke, Chris Troke, Ian Kendrick, Marion Kendrick (3 images)
"Camel racing at Sohar, after National Day" (3 images)
"Camel racing at Sohar, after National Day:" spectators, including Sayyid Thuwaini (Sultan's Representative) and Sayyid Badr (Minister of the Interior)(3 images)

"Camel racing at Sohar, after National Day:" spectators, including Sayyid Saif (Minister of the Diwan), Sheikh Khalid bin Saqr (Heir apparent of Ras al-Khaimah), AVM Talib bin Miran al-Raissi (CRAFO)(3 images)
"Camel racing at Sohar, after National Day:" spectators, unidentified (4 images)
"Sardine fishing at Aviation Beach" (3 images, of men pulling in nets)
"At Sinaw market" (2 images, of children)
Portrait of Sultan Qabus, standing outdoors, with mountains in background (1 image) and Prime Minister John Major, being greeted at arrival at airport, with Amb. Clark present (1 image)
"Campsite near Ja'alan" and "Mubarak, our guide/tracker in the Empty Quarter"
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Sheikh Said with islanders, children in Hallaniya" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): whales off Hallaniya, and Peter Holmes, Guy Loftus (PDO), Capt. Willy" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): limestone scarp (30 million years old) and turtle nests" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Al-Sawda Island, Al-Hallaniya (only inhabited village) entrance to the port from south, eastern tip of Al-Hallaniya Island" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): approaching Al Sawda Island from north," plus basket weavers at Shuwaimiya (4 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): basket weavers at Shuwaimiya" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Shuwaimiya embarcation point for Kuria Muria" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Wadi Adhailat" (4 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Limestone formations, Wadi Adhailat" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Wadi Adhailat [and one image copied from Thesiger, of guide and saluki](2 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): Umm al-Samim salt pans" (3 images)
"Expedition to the Kuria Muria Island (Hallaniyat Island): rig at al-Majhar gas field, and Sir Peter Holmes, Royal Dutch Shell, with Sheikh Said al-Kilbani, PDO (Petroleum Development Oman)(3 images)

"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "chez Sheikh Ahmed bin Saif al-Mahrooqi at Adam," including Salim (driver), Kemal Sultan, Sheikh Ahmed, Ian Kendrick (RAFO), Betty Dalrymple, John Dalrymple, Denise Troke, Marion Kendrick (3 images)
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Muhammad al-Jardani, friend of Kemal Sultan; Harsusi beduin, Jiddat al-Harasis" (3 images)
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : Harsusi bedouin women
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Goat-herd-Jiddat al-Harasis" (4 images)
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Harsusi beduin in the Huqf" (3 images)
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Triliths in the Huqf" and "Jenaba boy at Sirab" (4 images)
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Jenaba beduin at Sirab" and "Harsusi beduin in the Huqf" (4 images)
"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Harsusi beduin in the Huqf" (3 images)

"Visit to al-Wusta region" : "Najma [Clark's saluki dog] with camels watering near camp" (2 images) and "John Bawtree and his sketch of the embassy (1 images) and unlabeled image of fortresses surrounding Muscat harbor (1 image)
Scenes in old Muscat : "Matrah waterfront, Zawawi mosque [in] old Muscat, entrance to Muscat bay, Bait Nasib, Muscat" (3 images)
Scenes in old Muscat : "Muscat lighthouse, gunroom in Fort Jalali, Jalali [depiction of objects such as dallah, khanjar, and other items on display](3 images)
Scenes in old Muscat : "The Embassy and residence verandah" (2 images) and "General John Graham, ex-Commander, Sultan's Armed Forces, on the verandah [and] Rosemary Graham" (1 image)

"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : "First a puncture! ...with John Shipman reflected in [the] glasses [of] the 'tour operator,' Abdul Hakim al-Yahmadi, manager of Muscat Bank at Mintirib" and images of oryx in field (3 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : "our guide, Said Sultan, saddling up" and others (one picture removed, 4 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : "Qasim, Nasr [two boys], Said baking bread in the ashes, Qasim with Najma [saluki] at breakfast" (6 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : "Said baking bread in the ashes" (1 image)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : unlabeled, image of Bedouin man and image of seated boy [perhaps Qasim](2 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : unlabeled image of camels, one man riding camel, young man or boy leading camel, and two saluki dogs with camel (4 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : "Mubarak" [young man, seated], unlabeled iimage of Mubarak with saluki dog, two people with recumbent camel (3 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : unlabeled images of people, including Bedouin man, with children, camels, salukis (3 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : unlabeled images of boy with salukis, Mubarak with recumbent camel, and camels with salukis (3 images)
"Camel trekking in the Wahaiba Sands" : unlabeled images of man with camel kneeling to graze from shrub, Mubarak seated with camel, and Mubarak and boy alongside a Toyota pickup truck (3 images)

"Visit to the farm of Mahmood Naser al-Mahrzi at Wadi Tayin...here with his medical student daughter, Bushra" (2 images) and "Niagara Falls!" [image of rain pouring off of roof of veranda at Embassy residence]. One removed image.
Unlabeled images, one of unidentified harbor, and one of grounds of Sultan's palace (Qasr al Alam) in old Muscat, and adjacent buildings, including al-Mirani fort (2 images)
"Visit to the farm of Mahmood Naser al-Mahrzi at Wadi Tayin" : "Jebel al-Kanisa (Church Hill) near Wadi Tayin, scarecrow [at] Wadi 'Umq" (3 images)
"Return to Mintirib" : people seated on ground, drinking coffee and reading document, including father of Said bin Sultan (3 images)
"Return to Mintirib" : "Obaida with Chinese jar, Said bin Sultan, date pollinating" (3 images)
"Omani air force pipers at Bertilsson's [Swedish ambassador] farewell" (3 images)

"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "Sheikh Ahmed bin Saif al Mahruqi at Adam with sons and brother with daughter, Abudall bin Saif al Mahruqi and Ian Kendrick, circular tower (?) at Sallout near Bisya" (3 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "Sallout, Sulaif near Ibri" (3 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "Salima and Liese [Mrs. Clark] at Wadi Aswad, Marion Kendrick" (3 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : unlabeled images of women and children, seated in room, meeting with foreign guests (3 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "Sheikh Saif bin Salim al-Dari wearing Julian Lush's hat," "Salima," with images of latter seated in room and pouring coffee for guests (5 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "Shaikh Saif bin Salim al-Dari'" and "a neighbor (owner of 'Saddah' the old saluqi)" (4 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "Nasir bin Saif" and image of his father, Sheikh Saif bin Salim, and "beehive tombs at Bat" (3 images)
"On tour at 'Id al-Fitr" : "beehive tombs at Bat" (3 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "crossing Wadi Shab," "Tom King [and] Michael Stoddart" and "Wadi Tiwi" (3 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "goats near Qalbat," "Tom King with our host at Sur, Sa'ad Bahwan" (3 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "master boatbuilder, Sur" (4 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "Sa'ad's cousin, Muhammad," "the ferry between Sur and al-Aija" (4 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "Sur from al-Aija," "the fort overlooking Sur," "weaver at al-Aija," "Wali's fort at Bilad Sur" (4 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "inside Sa'ad Bahwan's family house, Sur," "Beduin women at Mintirib," "Obaida at Mintirib" (4 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "Saif bin Hashil al-Maskery at Ibra," separate images of him seated with Tom King and Jane King (2 images)
"Visit of Tom and Jane King (ex-Minister of Defence, Easter 1993" : "Abdallah bin Hashil al-Maskery," seated with Tom and Jane King, and "Khamis Salim Ali al-Sunnaidi"

Various : "Khamis who just dropped in on us," "Dr. Omar al-Zawawi at the races," "Amhad Said al-Shanfari, Minister of Petroleum, at the races" and unlabeled images of a man riding a team of horses while standing on the saddle (5 images)
Various : at the races, with horses pulling a carriage, individuals riding horses, and group of camels with men standing in front (4 images)
Various : at the races (3 images)
Various : at the races (3 images)
"Jebel trip" : scenery of mountains and campsite (3 images)
"Jebel trip" : "fossils," "woodcutter," "feral donkeys," and image of sunset over mountain and flowers (5 images)
"Jebel trip" : "reservoir and stone house on the Jebel" and "looking down on Bilad Sait, Wadi Bani Kharous" (2 images)
Album 7 : "Oman, 1993"
"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : "visit to Nizwa suq" and "Chris Troke" (4 images)
"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : "Hilal (liaison officer, Jebel Akhdar), Tonia Goddard," "Salim at the rose water maker's, Saiq" (5 images)
"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : "Suleiman bin Himyar's cave (large enough to hide his rebel army, 1957" and "Shuraija, Jebel Akhdar" (4 images)
"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : "On board Dr. Omar al-Zawawi's dhow, Arus al-Bahar" (4 images)
"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : "On board Dr. Omar al-Zawawi's dhow, Arus al-Bahar," musicians playing drums and oud (5 images)

"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : images on Dr. al-Zawawi's dhow (2 images) and meeting with "Gen. Ali Majid al-Maameri, MInister of Palace Affairs, Gen. Abdul Alim," with Ambassador Clark and Marshal Harding (1 image)
Unlabeled, meeting of Ambassador Clark and Marshal Harding with General al-Maameri (1 image) and "AGM of British Scholarships for Oman (BSO)" (1 image)
"BSO [British Scholarships for Oman] awards," images of Ambassador Clark presenting awards to recipients (4 images)

"Visit of Sir Michael Burton, AUSS / FCO" : with Sheikh Khalaf al-Kharusi at Nakhl and Sheikh Rashid bin Hamdan at Suwaiq, plus one unidentified image of Ambassador Clark at diplomatic event (6 images)

"Visit of Marshal of the Royal Air Force, Sir Peter Harding" : "bullfight at Suwaiq," "saving plans from a log at Sur," and unlabeled image of Ambassador Clark with Sheikh Rashid bin Hamdan al-Saladi (4 images)
"Harsusi woman, Wahaiba Sands" and "Sheikh Saif bin Salim al-Dari', and his children, Wadi Aswad" (6 images)
"Sheikh Saif bin Salim's children" and "Falaj and pool at Wadi Dhayiqa" (5 images)
Unidentified Omani men and horses, and "Dancers in Quriyat" (6 images)
"Dancers celebrating National Heritage Day in Quriyat" (4 images)
"Visit of Gen. Mike Wilkes, M.E. Advisor to CDS, dinner at Sayyid Fahr's" (4 images)
"Visit of Gen. Mike Wilkes, M.E. Advisor to CDS, dinner at Sayyid Fahr's" (6 images)
"Visit of the Defence Secretary, Malcolm Rifkind" : "in Nizwa silver suq" (5 images)
"Visit of the Defence Secretary, Malcolm Rifkind" : "Nizwa fort" (4 images)

"Visit of the Defence Secretary, Malcolm Rifkind" : includes talking with member of the press, signing guest book at Nizwa Fort, with Hilal (liaison officer at Jebel Akhdar), visiting Saiq, and with Philip Goddard and Ambassador Clark at luncheon hosted by Sayyid Fahr (6 images)
Visit to Ibra : "mountains near Ibra" and "entrance to the old town of Manzfa, Ibra" (4 images)
Visit to Ibra : "Bait al-Sudoor, Manzfa (18th century)," "the old souq, Manzfa" and "Khalfan bin Salim bin Saleem al-Na'mani of Manzfa, a former hunter with Saluqis" (4 images)
Visit to Ibra : "Manzfa," "watchtower at Ibra," "old man at Manzfa," and "Shaikh Said bin Ahmad al-Kindy at his horse farm at Amerat" (4 images)
"Tombs near Jabrin, circa 2000 BC" (5 images)
Tombs near Jabrin : "arrow heads," "soapstone container," "finds in copper from the tombs," plus images of group with Amb. Clark exploring the ruins (6 images)
"At Wadi Abyadh" [group portrait] (2 images) and "Folk dancers at Nizwa at 'Id al Fitr" (3 images)
"Bahla potteries" and "the kiln" (4 images)
"Visit to Masirah" : "our C-130 transport," "road from BERS to RAFO," "Shaikh Khalifa, Shaikh of Masirah," "Sh. Abdallah al-Shobani (from Ibri), Wali of Masirah" (5 images)
"Visit to Masirah" : "ruins of a Portuguese fort," "Fisherman at Qitaara landing a kingfish weighing 23 kgs," and images of fishermen (6 images)
"Visit to Masirah" : various images of fisherman and their catch, at Qitaara (5 images)
"Visit to Masirah" : "at Sh. Khalifa's house, Half, Masirah," includes images of the sheikh's wife, daughter-in-law, and children, and Mrs. Clark, along with one image of a camel spider (5 images)
Visit to Salalah and Dhofar : "Gateway to the Palace-al Hisn-Salalah" (1 image), "the old souq in Salalah" (4 images), including men drinking coconut milk, shopkeepers
Visit to Salalah and Dhofar : "faces in the souq" (4 images), "Dhofari wedding headdress" (1 image)
Visit to Salalah and Dhofar : historical site of al-Balid in Salalah (4 images), al-Ayoun pools (1 image), and frankincense tree, with Dr. Juris Zarins and Nick Browne (FCO)
Visit to Salalah and Dhofar : "frankincense tree in flower" (2 images), "Jebali kraii" (1 image), and picnic that includes Muhammed Said Muhsin al-Mashali and his cousin, Musallam (2 images)
Visit to Salalah and Dhofar : "frankincense trees," "Ubar" [ruins], and Dr. Juris Zarins with Sheikh Musallam (Naib Wali, at Shisr), with one other man
Picture of a Kathiri and a Rashidi at Shisr (2 images), Gen. Khamis al-Kilbani (2 images), and Sheikh Ahmed bin Sultan al-Hosni, with horses at Fulaij (2 images)
"Visit to 'Coleman's' Rock near al-Hamra" [rock with petroglyphs and carvings](4 images)
"Visit to Adam for 'Id al-Adha festivities" [horse and camel racing] (5 images)
"Visit to Adam for 'Id al-Adha festivities" [horse and camel racing] (6 images)
Two men: "Talib, son of Shekh Muhammad bin Abdulla bin Zahir al-Hinai at Ghafat" and "Sheikh Amor bin Shwain al-Hosni at al-Khabourah" (3 images)
Unlabeled : Prince Philip (Duke of Edinburgh) with Ambassador Clark, and Omani Foreign Minister Yusuf Alawi (?) at British Council facility, likely in Muscat (2 images)
"Visit to Adam for 'Id al-Adha festivities" [horse and camel racing] (5 images)
"Visit to Adam for 'Id al-Adha festivities" [horse and camel racing] (5 images)
"Visit to Adam for 'Id al-Adha festivities" [horse and camel racing] (3 images)
Album 8 : "Muscat, Embassy, 1993"
Muscat Bay [view of old town of Muscat from hill behind town]
Plaque, reading: "Site of original flag pole used for manumission until 1963 and as an aid to navigation until dismantled 1973"
Building with flagpole, caption on verso : "The famous Embassy flagpole was dismantled in 1973 and this flagpole was erected on the Embassy roof"
Entry way, caption on verso : "Embassy entrance, built in 1890"
Walkway with cannon, caption on verso : "Cannon in Embassy entrance"
Caption : "Entrance to Muscat Bay with MENAS ship "Relume"
View from above of buildings along bayfront, Muscat, caption on verso : "Embassy in foreground, from above"
Exterior, chair on ground with cannon in background, caption on verso : "Sedan chair used for carrying VIPs ashore in Muscat bay (though this example is from the Embassy in Bahrain)"

Wall with plaques, and bell hung from ledge mounted to wall, caption on verso : "The BI 'Dolphin' [?] bell in the Embassy courtyard from the wreck after the ship was sunk by a Japanese torpedo from a submarine on 28 June 1943"
Helicopter on pad, overlooking bay, caption on verso : "Helipad at the side of the Embassy on Fort Jalali"
View from above of buildings along bayfront [identical to image on page 7]
Minaret with dome on top, caption on verso : "The Zawawi mosque in old Muscat"
View of British Embassy building with veranda, as seen from above (no caption)
White structure at base of mountain, caption on verso : "The foreigners' cemetery near the Embassy at Waljat"
Foreigners' cemetery, tombstones, seen from distance
Foreigners' cemetery, tombstones, as seen from closeup
Foreigners' cemetery: tombstone for Major Alfred Cotton Way (died 1871)
Foreigners' cemetery : various views
Caption on verso : "The Residence main reception room"
Caption on verso : "The Residence outer verandah"
Stairway, with wall lined with framed portraits, caption on verso : "Stairs to the Residence with the 'Rogue's Gallery'"
Inner courtyard, balustrades, with palms, caption on verso : "The Residence inner verandah"
Aerial view of mountain with buildings at base, bay in background, caption on verso : "Embassy from above"
Building on left, walkway with seafront on right, Sultan's palace (al-Alam palace) in background, caption on verso : "The Embassy seafront"
Portal with closed doors, man in Omani national dress standing in front, caption on verso : "Entrance to the Embassy compound"
Embassy and surrounding buildings, tower of Fort Jalali and adjacent helipad, seen from above, looking from sea, caption on verso : "The Embassy by Fort Jalali"
Muscat waterfront, looking southward, from above, with al-Alam Palace and British Embassy on right, Fort Jalali at left (no caption on verso)
Two buildings at right angle, caption on verso : "Bait Nasib on the right, where the Consular Section was located in old Muscat. The US Embassy is at the end of the lane"
Caption on verso : "Antique chest on Embassy verandah"
Ceremony, with Ambassador Clark, and others, lowering a time capsule into the courtyard of the newly constructed British Embassy [al Khuwair, Muscat] (5 images)
Construction of the new British Embassy [al Khuwair, Muscat] (8 images)
The new British Embassy [al Khuwair, Muscat], upon completion
Photographic slides
Places
Matrah (1965), entrance to British embassy and Mirani Fort in 1973, Old Ghafat, Bandar Jissah, Wadi Ghoul from February 1990, (20 slides)
Muscat (Sultan's Palace), Awabi, Nizwa, Wadi Ghoul (20 slides)
Wadi Ghoul (cont.), Chancery (Muscat), Salalah, Dhofar, Empty Quarter, Shisur
Wadi Bani Habib (Jebel Akhdar), J. Harasis, Musandam (20 slides)
Sur, Muscat, Musandam, Qalhat (20 slides)
Wahiba Sands, Suwaib, Wadi Bani Habib (Jebel Akhdar), Lekhwan oil field, Dhofar (Raysut, coastline)(20 slides)
Dhofar (Mughsail, Khor Khorfat, Wadi Darbat, Ayoun, Jebel Dhofar, desert)(20 slides)
Shisur, Rub' al-Khali, Shwaimiya, Wadi Adhailat (20 slides)
Wadi Adhailat, Shwaimiyah, al-Sawdah island, Kuria Muria Island, al-Halaniyat (20 slides)
Kuria Muria, al-Halaniyat, Ibra, Nakhl Fort (20 slides)
Balad Bani bu 'Ali, Jebel Akhdar, Ras al-Hadd, Adam, Muscat, including English Cemetery, British Embassy and Residence (20 slides)
Muscat, British Embassy and Residence, English cemetery (20 slides)
Sur, Dariz (Ibri), Wadi Hajir (20 slides)
Sur, al-Hamra, Jebel [Shams?] (20 slides)

Old Muscat, new British Embassy, Manzfa, New ambassador's residence [Al Bustan] (20 slides)
Jabrin, Wadi Batha, Wadi Tiwi, Sur, Qalhat, Misfat (20 slides)
Various locations (Dhofar, Musandam, Khasab, Muscat) and various types of regional boats (18 slides)
People
Fishermen below chancery (Muscat), Sheikh Muhammed's son at Ghafat, boys and camel at Wahibah Sands, men at Nizwa, Ghafat, Wadi Ghoul and Wadi Azayat, Dhofaris, Bedu at Shisur and Fasad (20 slides)
Jebel Akhdar (woman, card players, children), Harsusi ranger, fisherman at Musandam, Sheikh Saif Sultan and family (Wadi Aswad), date packers at Samail (20 slides)
Wadi Bani Kharus (spinner, children, men), Jebalis in Dhofar, various individuals (20 slides)
Dhofar (women spinning, child), Jebel Akhdar (drummers), Shwaimiyah (basket weavers), Shahra islander (20 slides)
Adam (various men), Shishur (sheikhs), Sur (traditional weavers), Wahiba Sands (bedouins, boys with camel), others (18 slides)
Musandam (singers), Sha'am (weavers), Wahibah (dancers and musicians), Nizwa (merchants), Wadi Tiwi (women), and others (14 slides)
Wilfred Thesiger visit, Muscat; including meeting with his former traveling companions on verandah of British Embassy residence (12 images)
Wilfred Thesiger visit, in Birkat al-Mauz with Bin Kabina, Bin Ghubaisha, and Musallim bin Tafl; and subsequent visit with Musallim bin Tafl in Mughshin (14 slides)
Various (10 slides)
Loose photographs and ephemera

Terence Clark, photograph in his office, British Embassy, Baghdad (1 print)
Terence Clark, official portrait in dress uniform, Muscat (1 print)
